seoTitle: Copy and Paste Website Funnels in GoHighLevel (Part 2)
metaDescription: A closer look at duplicating website funnels in GoHighLevel to reuse designs and save time on client and campaign builds.
intro: This tutorial continues the topic of duplicating website funnels in GoHighLevel, showing how to reuse existing designs rather than rebuilding pages from scratch for every new project. It covers the practical steps for copying, pasting, and customizing funnel pages within your account.

steps.heading: Open the funnel you want to duplicate
steps.body: Start by locating the existing funnel or website page in GoHighLevel that you want to copy.

steps.heading: Copy the funnel pages
steps.body: Use GoHighLevel's copy tools to duplicate the pages you need, whether for a new client or a variation of an existing campaign.

steps.heading: Paste and customize
steps.body: Paste the copied pages into their new location and adjust the branding, text, and images for the new use case.

steps.heading: Publish the updated funnel
steps.body: Once customized, publish the duplicated funnel so it's ready to use for the new project or campaign.

steps.heading: Keep a library of reusable designs
steps.body: Maintain a set of funnels you can reuse and adapt going forward, saving time on future builds.

faq.question: How is this different from copying a single page?
faq.answer: This tutorial focuses on the practical mechanics of copying and pasting funnels, which can apply to individual pages or full funnel structures.

faq.question: Can I reuse a funnel across different clients?
faq.answer: Yes, customizing copied funnels for different clients or campaigns is a common use case for this workflow.

faq.question: Do I need to rebuild branding each time I copy a funnel?
faq.answer: You'll typically need to update branding, text, and images for the new use case, but the underlying structure carries over.

faq.question: Does this save significant time compared to building from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes, reusing existing funnel designs is generally much faster than building new pages from the ground up.

faq.question: Who would use this workflow regularly?
faq.answer: Agencies and freelancers managing multiple client funnels are the most likely to benefit from this repeatable process.
